nameMeanings.name: Mainwaring
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mesnil-Varin in Saint-Paër (Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie). The placename means ‘domain of Warin’ (see   Waring  ). Compare   Manwaring   and   Mandry  .
Irish: adopted for Ó Manaráin, see Mannering 2.

variants.name: Manwaring
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mainwaring  .

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Randall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randell
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randal
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randle
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randel
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Randall Mainwaring was born in 1425, in Congleton, Cheshire, England, his father, Randall Mainwaring, was 11 and his mother, Elizabeth Savage, was 11. He had at least 2 sons with Margaret Savage. He died in 1522, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Randall Mainwaring est né en 1425, à Congleton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Randall Mainwaring, avait 11 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Savage, avait 11 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Margaret Savage. Il est décédé en 1522, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 97 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Randall Mainwaring 1425 in Congleton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Randall Mainwaring 11 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Savage 11. Er hatte mit Margaret Savage mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1522 im Alter von 97 Jahren in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Randall Mainwaring è nato il nel 1425 nel Congleton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Randall Mainwaring, aveva 11 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Savage, aveva 11 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Margaret Savage. Morì nel 1522, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 97 anni.
